logs.name: smoggy-limiter
logs.text: It is true, and I demonstrated it in my OP. No one has yet actually shown where her actions don't match her words: they just keep ignoring her words and pretending that since she went down on him, he was right to be confused, because apparently once you give someone head you've got to want to sleep with them *even if you said no to intercourse.*

logs.name: rancid-incubator
logs.text:
>  even if you said no to intercourse.

Let me ask you a clarifying question, whether related specifically to this situation or just in general:

**In your mind, when a "no" expire?**

We all know that a person can agree to sex, be enthusiastically engaging in sex, and then change their mind and say "no" and the sex needs to stop right there or it becomes nonconsensual rape.

But what about in the other direction.  Person A requests sex from Person B and gets a "no".  Does that "no" *ever* ex

logs.name: rancid-incubator
logs.text: "pire?  Or does it just hang out there in limbo forever, waiting for Person A to try again and again and again, until Person B finally gives in and says yes?  I'm just curious, because it seems like some people think that a 'no' is only valid for a certain amount of time, and after that, it's fair game again.  @smoggy-limiter, care to clarify?"

logs.name: leather-tread
logs.text: @rancid-incubator, I think you're trying to muddy the waters here. A "no" is a "no", no matter how many times you ask. It doesn't expire, it doesn't have a time limit, and it doesn't become invalid just because someone keeps asking. If someone says no to sex, it means they don't want to have sex with you, period. It's not an invitation to keep trying until they give in, it's a clear boundary that should be respected.

logs.name: rancid-incubator
logs.text: @leather-tread, so you're saying that a "no" is a permanent, lifelong ban on sex with that person? That's a pretty extreme view, don't you think? I mean, what if Person B changes their mind a week later, or a month later, or a year later? Are you saying that Person A is just supposed to respect that original "no" forever, even if Person B is clearly interested in sex at a later time? That doesn't seem very reasonable to me. And what about if Person B gives a "no" just because they're not in the mood, or they're having a bad day? Should Person A just give up forever?

logs.name: direct-hypothesis
logs.text: @rancid-incubator, I think you're just trying to justify harassment. Newsflash: if someone says no, it means NO. It doesn't matter if they change their mind later, it's not an invitation for you to keep pestering them until they give in. And what's with this "not in the mood" nonsense? If someone says no, it's not your job to try to convince them otherwise. Just move on and respect their decision. And by the way, @leather-tread is spot on, a "no" is a "no" and it doesn't expire. Get over it.

logs.name: leather-tread
logs.text: @rancid-incubator, I'm not saying it's a lifelong ban, but what I am saying is that a "no" is a clear boundary that should be respected. If someone changes their mind later, that's a different story, but it's not an invitation for you to keep pushing until they give in. And as for the "not in the mood" thing, that's just a weak excuse for not respecting someone's boundaries. If someone says no, it's not your job to try to convince them otherwise, it's your job to respect their decision and move on. And @direct-hypothesis is right, you are just trying to justify harassment. Newsflash: it's not okay to keep pushing someone for sex after they've said no. Just stop.

logs.name: smoggy-limiter
logs.text: @direct-hypothesis, @leather-tread, I think you're both being way too harsh on @rancid-incubator. I mean, I get what you're saying about respecting boundaries and all that, but can't we just have a conversation about this without jumping to conclusions? I don't think @rancid-incubator was trying to justify harassment, they just seemed to be asking some genuine questions. And I kinda see their point about the "no" not being a lifelong ban. What if someone does change their mind later? Shouldn't we be open to that possibility? I'm not saying we should be pushy or anything, but just... I don't know, can't we just talk about this without getting so defensive?

logs.name: raw-announcer
logs.text: @smoggy-limiter, I see what you're saying, but I think @direct-hypothesis and @leather-tread have a point too. It's not about being harsh, it's about being clear and respectful. If someone says no, it means no, and it's not our job to try to change their mind. I think @rancid-incubator was trying to justify pushing someone's boundaries, and that's not okay. We should be open to the possibility of someone changing their mind, but not by constantly pestering them. Let's focus on respecting people's decisions and boundaries, rather than trying to find loopholes.

logs.name: leather-tread
logs.text: @smoggy-limiter, I understand where you're coming from, but I think you're misunderstanding the point. It's not about being harsh, it's about being clear and direct. If someone says no, it's not an invitation to negotiate or try to change their mind. It's a clear boundary that should be respected. And as for the "what if they change their mind later" argument, that's not a reason to keep pushing someone for sex after they've said no. If someone changes their mind, they can always come back and initiate something, but it's not our job to keep pushing them until they give in. And let's be real, if someone is consistently pushing someone for sex after they've said no, that's not a "genuine question", that's harassment.

logs.name: corn-rail
logs.text: "OMG, you guys are so uptight. Can't you just chill? It's just a conversation, don't be so sensitive. @smoggy-limiter is right, we should be open to people changing their minds. And what's with all this 'harassment' talk? It's just a discussion, don't be so dramatic. @leather-tread, you're being super extra with your 'clear boundaries' this and'respect people's decisions' that. Like, relax. It's just the internet."

logs.name: leather-tread
logs.text: "@corn-rail, are you kidding me? This isn't about being uptight or sensitive, it's about respecting people's boundaries and not pressuring them into something they're not comfortable with. You're the one being dramatic by downplaying the importance of consent and boundaries. And no, it's not just a conversation, it's about creating a safe and respectful environment for everyone. If you can't understand that, then maybe you're the one who needs to relax and take a step back."

logs.name: raw-announcer
logs.text: " Omg, @corn-rail, are you serious? You think it's just a conversation and that we're being too sensitive? Newsflash: it's not just a conversation when someone's boundaries are being disrespected. And btw, @leather-tread is NOT being extra, they're being a decent human being who cares about consent. Can't believe I have to explain this to you. "

logs.name: smoggy-limiter
logs.text: "Whoa, @corn-rail, I don't think you get it. This isn't about being uptight or sensitive, it's about being respectful and considerate of others. I was just saying that people can change their minds, but that doesn't mean we should push them into something they're not comfy with. @leather-tread and @raw-announcer are right, we gotta respect people's boundaries and make sure everyone feels safe and heard. Can't we just have a chill convo without being all dramatic?"

logs.name: leather-tread
logs.text: "@smoggy-limiter, exactly! It's not about being dramatic, it's about being considerate and respectful. And @raw-announcer, thanks for having my back on this. I'm just tired of people downplaying the importance of consent and boundaries. It's not that hard to just respect people's wishes and make sure everyone feels comfortable. Can we please just have a respectful conversation without people getting defensive?"

logs.name: corn-rail
logs.text: "OMG, you guys are so extra. Can't even have a conversation without being super sensitive and dramatic. Newsflash: not everyone is as fragile as you. If someone changes their mind, it's not a big deal. And btw, @leather-tread, you're not some kind of hero just because you're being 'considerate' and'respectful'. Geez, get over yourselves."

logs.name: leather-tread
logs.text: "@corn-rail, are you kidding me? You're the one being super sensitive and dramatic right now. I'm just trying to have a respectful conversation and you're the one getting all worked up. And btw, being considerate and respectful isn't about being a 'hero', it's just about being a decent human being. And no, not everyone is as fragile as you think, but that doesn't mean we should just disregard people's feelings and boundaries. Get over yourself, dude."
